summary:
Claim: Sir Frederick Sanger discovered the amino acid sequence of insulin and identified interspecies structural differences.  
Evidence: - He determined insulin’s full amino acid sequence through chemical analysis in the 1940s–1950s; - His work revealed key variations between human and bovine insulin, proving functional significance of minor structural changes.  
Answer: B
formatted_summary:
agent2 (Round 2 summarized): Claim: Sir Frederick Sanger discovered the amino acid sequence of insulin and identified interspecies structural differences.  
Evidence: - He determined insulin’s full amino acid sequence through chemical analysis in the 1940s–1950s; - His work revealed key variations between human and bovine insulin, proving functional significance of minor structural changes.  
Answer: B. Answer choice: B
